label: Brain and central nervous system
quick_take: The most important caveat to anything about brain cancer in CI5 is that registries before vol VII may have included benign tumours. Many "rises" in vol V–VI then "falls" in vol VII are almost certainly reclassification artefacts. Setting that aside, the *real* findings concern Israel, Indian registries, and a persistent East-African drop after vol X.

findings.title: Israel Jews born in Africa / Asia women, vol V: ASR 16.58, 4.1× the world median
findings.text: Israel Jews born in Israel women also 16.05 in vol III. These are historical highs and consistent with reports of radiation-for-tinea-capitis treatment in North African immigrant cohorts (1940s–50s childhood exposure showing up decades later).
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Israel Jews born in Africa / Asia women, vol V: ASR 16.58, 4.1× the world median

findings.title: Inverted-U in Israel Jewish women
findings.text: peaks vol III (16.05) and falls to 4.04 by vol VIII — 71 % down as the exposed cohorts pass.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Inverted-U in Israel Jewish women

findings.title: India Trivandrum men: U-shape 11.62 → 2.44 → 2.85
findings.text: Another registry where vol VII → VIII is a scope change, not biology.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — India Trivandrum men: U-shape 11.62 → 2.44 → 2.85

findings.title: Multiple registries show a "vol VI-peak and decline" pattern
findings.text: USA LA Japanese men 3.06 → peak → 6.71 back to 6.71; USA NM American Indian men peaked 8.35 in vol III. Many of these sit near the benign-tumour coding boundary.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Multiple registries show a "vol VI-peak and decline" pattern

findings.title: Rising African rates: Algeria Sétif +931 % women, +1375 % men; Uganda Kyadondo +583 % women
findings.text: From tiny baselines but upward, consistent with improving imaging access. Baselines are 1–8 cases per volume (SMALL_N) and pop_warning Uganda vol VII; % changes are inflated by the small denominators. Direction is consistent, magnitudes are not precise.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Rising African rates: Algeria Sétif +931 % women, +1375 % men; Uganda Kyadondo +583 % women

findings.title: Bimodal age curves in large US, Belarus and Croatia samples
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Bimodal age curves in large US, Belarus and Croatia samples

findings.title: Peak-age 1 in Israel Jews-born-in-Africa/Asia women
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Peak-age 1 in Israel Jews-born-in-Africa/Asia women

findings.title: cancer_warning
findings.text: brain and CNS may include benign tumours before vol VII. This is the single biggest interpretation hazard in the dataset — any "vol VI → vol VII drop" must be caveated.
Many registries changed imaging practice (CT, MRI) and coding at different times; cross-registry levels are not robust.
India Trivandrum vol VII → VIII step is registry-scope change.

findings.title: Israel Jews-from-Africa/Asia women
findings.text: a cohort paper on the declining brain-tumour burden from post-WW2 tinea-capitis irradiation. The numbers are big enough to support it.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Israel Jews-from-Africa/Asia women

findings.text: A systematic vol-VI-to-VII comparison across registries would reveal which countries cleaned up benign tumour coding — a methods-heavy but useful IARC internal paper.
